analysis.problem_statement: Release notes are inconsistent, manual, and often delayed, leading to miscommunication and inefficiencies within product teams.
analysis.business_goal: To streamline the release notes process, ensuring consistency and timeliness while reducing the manual workload for product and engineering managers.
constraints: Must generate notes from existing meeting notes and Jira summaries
assumptions: Users have access to structured meeting notes and Jira summaries
AI can accurately interpret and summarize the provided data
open_questions: What level of detail is required in the release notes?
How will users provide feedback on the generated notes?
What specific formats or templates do users prefer for release notes?

draft.title: AI-Generated Release Notes
draft.problem_statement: Release notes are inconsistent, manual, and often delayed, leading to miscommunication and inefficiencies within product teams.
goals: Ensure consistency and timeliness of release notes
Reduce manual workload for product and engineering managers
non_goals: Create release notes without any input from users
Replace all manual processes related to product management

user_stories.story: As a product manager, I want to generate release notes from meeting notes and Jira summaries so that I can save time and ensure consistency.
acceptance_criteria: Release notes are generated from structured meeting notes and Jira summaries
Generated notes require minimal manual editing
Users can review and approve the generated notes before distribution

risks: AI may misinterpret data leading to inaccurate release notes
Users may not have structured meeting notes or Jira summaries available
success_metrics: Reduction in time spent creating release notes
Increase in user satisfaction with the release notes process
Decrease in the number of revisions required post-generation
open_questions: What level of detail is required in the release notes?
How will users provide feedback on the generated notes?
What specific formats or templates do users prefer for release notes?

strengths: Identified target users and user stories
Defined success metrics that are relevant to the goals
gaps: Acceptance criteria could be more detailed and specific
Metrics lack quantifiable targets (e.g., specific percentage reduction in time)
Risks do not cover potential user resistance or training needs
Dependencies are not fully elaborated (e.g., what if access is denied?)
improvements: Enhance acceptance criteria with specific examples of acceptable release notes
Add quantifiable targets to success metrics (e.g., reduce time by 50%)
Include additional risks related to user adoption and training requirements
Clarify dependencies with contingency plans in case of access issues

finalPrd.title: AI-Generated Release Notes
finalPrd.problem_statement: Release notes are inconsistent, manual, and often delayed, leading to miscommunication and inefficiencies within product teams.
goals: Ensure consistency and timeliness of release notes
Reduce manual workload for product and engineering managers
non_goals: Create release notes without any input from users
Replace all manual processes related to product management

user_stories.story: As a product manager, I want to generate release notes from meeting notes and Jira summaries so that I can save time and ensure consistency.
acceptance_criteria: Release notes are generated from structured meeting notes and Jira summaries
Generated notes require minimal manual editing, with examples of acceptable formats provided
Users can review and approve the generated notes before distribution, with a clear approval process outlined

risks: AI may misinterpret data leading to inaccurate release notes
Users may not have structured meeting notes or Jira summaries available
Potential user resistance to adopting the new process
Training may be required for users to effectively utilize the tool

dependencies.contingency_plan: Identify alternative data sources or manual entry options if access is denied

success_metrics: Reduction in time spent creating release notes by 50%
Increase in user satisfaction with the release notes process by 30%
Decrease in the number of revisions required post-generation by 40%
open_questions: What level of detail is required in the release notes?
How will users provide feedback on the generated notes?
What specific formats or templates do users prefer for release notes?
